meta.title: Kevin Andrew — quiet systems for loud problems
meta.description: Kevin Andrew builds cloud infrastructure, protocols, and applied AI — designed from first principles and shipped end to end.

home.lede: I run on caffeine, playlists, and "what if?" Most days you'll find me designing, building, researching, or convincing myself that starting another side project is a good idea. If an idea keeps me awake at night, it's probably worth building.
home.workKeynote: A protocol. A pipeline. A paper.
home.workKeynoteBody: Three systems carried end to end — from the first noticing to a public artifact, each one written down so others can build on it.
home.featuredSub: STEM — cloning production without the blast radius
home.manifesto: Notice the problem. Interrogate it until it confesses structure. Build the system. Write it down. Repeat until it compounds.
home.doorsWritingBody: Mostly notes to myself. Maybe they'll help you too.
home.doorsResearchBody: Some problems need more than code. Formal work on provenance and resource allocation — and the open questions behind it.
home.doorsStudioBody: A small practice for people who need systems that simply work. Select client work, and how it runs.
home.now1: Building Etch — cryptographic provenance for code. Open-core, spec written in public.
home.now2: A paper on cloud resource allocation is under double-blind review.
home.now3: Edith Studio is open for select client engagements.

work.title: Work
work.subtitle: Here are the things that kept me busy when I probably should've been sleeping.
work.transition: Turns out **"just one more project"** is never just one.
work.archiveNote: Links land here as each project's public artifact is verified — nothing on this site ships a dead end.

aboutPage.short: The useful description is simple: I notice problems, interrogate them until they confess structure, build the system, and write it down. Right now that means a provenance protocol, an allocation research problem, and a small studio practice — run in parallel.
aboutPage.how1b: Templates answer questions nobody asked. Most of solving a problem is interrogating it until it confesses structure — the code that follows is almost dictation.
aboutPage.how2t: Demos are easy. Handover is the test
aboutPage.how2b: An idea is finished when someone else can run it without me in the room — and it still behaves at 3 a.m., under load, on a bad day.
aboutPage.how3t: Writing is a debugger for thinking
aboutPage.how3b: Every system ships with its explanation. If the explanation doesn't hold, the system was never understood — just assembled.
aboutPage.rooms: Some systems have people in them. Years of running rooms — chapters, committees, crews — taught the same lessons as engineering: set clear interfaces, remove friction, give people air cover, and let ownership do the rest. A room is a distributed system with feelings.
aboutPage.fieldworkNote: Domains crossed on the way here.
aboutPage.el1: Deep space — the aesthetic and the physics, both.
aboutPage.el3: Theology and physics — different instruments, same question.

contactPage.title: Let's build something that lasts.
contactPage.body: For client work, research, collaboration, or a question worth asking — the fastest route is email. Replies usually within a day.
